sources.title: Sleuth (1972 Thriller film) If it was murder, where's the body?  Laurence Olivier, Michael Cain
sources.description:
Two acting legends. One mansion. Zero rules.
Mystery writer Andrew Wyke lures his wife's new flame Milo Tindle into a twisted game at his tricked-out mansion. Their battle of wits escalates wildly as traps backfire and disguises drop, turning a playful face-off into a no-holds-barred showdown.

CASTLIST:
Laurence Olivier (Oscar®-nominated) - Andrew Wyke
Michael Cain (Oscar®-nominated) - Milo Tindle
Teddy Martin - Police Constable Higgs
Alec Cawthorne - Inspector Doppler (voice)
Joanne Woodward - Marguerite Wyke (portrait only)

CREDITS
Directed by 4x Oscar® winner Joseph L. Mankiewicz (All About Eve)
Based on Anthony Shaffer’s Tony-winning play
Cinematography by Oswald Morris • Production Design by Ken Adam (James Bond films)

ACCOLADES
🏆 4 Oscar® nominations (including BOTH leads!)
🏆 Edgar Award for Best Mystery Screenplay

metadata.Plot: Andrew Wyke is a famous and successful author of detective novels. Milo Tindle comes to him with a strange request, that Mr Wyke divorce his wife so that Tindle can marry her. Mr Wyke is not particularly perturbed by this, he and his wife have drifted apart, and he is having an affair with another woman anyway, but uses the meeting and Mr Tindle's request as a chance to play a game, a game with potentially deadly consequences.
metadata.Actors: Laurence Olivier, Michael Caine, Alec Cawthorne
metadata.Awards: Nominated for 4 Oscars. 5 wins & 11 nominations total
